scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Mira logs into Hillside's client portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Mira Sundar, CFO of Aster Capital, opens the Hillside client portal. Instead of accepting just a password, Maestro orchestrates a CLEAR identity verification — biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any transaction begins. Witness event #1.

beats.head: CLEAR session opens on Mira's phone.
beats.lede: No app download — a one-tap text link.

beats.head: ID + selfie + biometric match.
beats.lede: CLEAR runs the comparison; the result posts back to Maestro in seconds.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens.
beats.lede: Biometric proof bound to the session. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Mira initiates a $4M intra-account transfer; Maestro serves the authorization webform — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside her authenticated session, Mira initiates a $4M cash movement between two of Aster's accounts. The portal stays Hillside-branded; Maestro serves a transfer-authorization webform pre-filled from her IDV-bound identity and account context. No second IDV — the form inherits the session's biometric proof. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Mira opens "New Transfer" in the portal.
Maestro renders the authorization webform inline.
beats.lede: Pre-filled with from/to, amount, SWIFT, and the F1 identity binding.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens.
beats.lede: Anomaly check against Aster's normal pattern; no flag.

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established biometric proof.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Agreement Desk surfaces the CLEAR auth event and the transfer submission as one continuous identity thread.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been binding the auth event to the transaction event in the orchestration layer the whole time — Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Hillside's compliance team sees the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at portal entry, and the transfer submission — linked as a continuous identity thread. One audit trail. One defensible record. The chain-of-custody gap closes.

beats.head: Agreement Desk opens to Mira's session activity.
beats.lede: The auth event and the transfer event sit in a single view.

beats.head: The link is explicit.
beats.lede: "Verified by CLEAR · Mira Sundar · CA DL ***4892 · 13:42 EST" → "Transfer authorization · $4,000,000 · 13:47 EST".

beats.head: Iris confirms pattern match.
beats.lede: Risk score below threshold; Priya's compliance queue gets a normal-priority item.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If the transaction is ever disputed, Hillside has both halves of the chain.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Priya fills Hillside's new-engagement webform for Aster Capital.
scenes.lede: Priya Das, Head of Compliance at Hillside Advisors, opens the new-engagement webform and configures the engagement for Aster Capital: entity type (LLC), AUM tier ($100M+), discretionary management mandate, custodian (Pershing).

beats.head: Priya selects "New Engagement" from Hillside's intake menu.
She fills entity type, mandate, AUM tier.
beats.lede: Each selection branches what fields appear next.

beats.head: Reg BI disclosure auto-attaches.
beats.lede: Based on advisor type — no manual lookup.

beats.head: Priya reviews the configured package summary.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out IMA + custodial agreement + Reg BI from Priya's selections.
scenes.lede: The moment Priya submits the webform, Maestro orchestrates: it pulls the IMA template for the entity type and AUM tier, the Pershing custodial agreement, and the Reg BI disclosure. Each document pre-fills with Aster's KYB data. The recipient list is built. The envelope is ready.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the right IMA template.
beats.lede: LLC + discretionary at $100M+ tier = a specific contract variant.

beats.head: Aster's KYB record pre-fills 14 fields across the docs.
Recipient routing built.
beats.lede: Aster's authorized signatory first, Hillside compliance reviewer second.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Aster's signatory reviews and signs in a Hillside-branded ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packaged envelope arrives at Aster's authorized signatory in seconds. Hillside-branded email, signing ceremony with the IMA + custodial + Reg BI together, identity verified at signing time. Compliance review fires automatically.

beats.head: Hillside-branded email lands in Aster's signatory's inbox.
Ceremony opens with the three docs in order.
Signature applied across the three docs.
Multi-recipient routing: Hillside compliance reviewer next.
beats.lede: Priya's compliance officer receives the envelope automatically.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · ONGOING RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Hillside, Aster, and the custodian continue in one Workspace.
scenes.lede: Onboarding closes; the relationship Workspace opens. Aster, Pershing, and Hillside's compliance team now have a single shared surface for the engagement going forward — same activity log, same agreement set, same Iris.

beats.head: Aster's signatory gets a Workspace invite.
Engagement summary visible to all three parties.
Iris is in the rail.
beats.lede: Activity log shows the full Maestro fan-out.

beats.head: KYC checks running in the background.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Jordan logs into Riverside's patient portal; Maestro orchestrates DEA-grade CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Jordan Kim opens Riverside Health's patient portal. For patients with active controlled-substance prescriptions, Riverside requires DEA-grade identity proofing on every session. Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login — biometric proof established before any clinical action begins. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Jordan hits Riverside's patient portal login.
Maestro detects active Schedule II Rx on file.
CLEAR session opens on Jordan's phone.
Authenticated session opens.
beats.lede: Biometric proof bound to the session. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Jordan requests a Schedule II refill; Maestro serves the refill-request webform — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside the authenticated session, Jordan opens "Refill Prescription." Maestro renders the refill-request webform inline — pre-filled with the active prescription, prescriber, dispensing pharmacy, and the F1 IDV identity binding. No second IDV. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Jordan opens "Refill Prescription" in the portal.
beats.lede: Schedule II Rx selected from the active list.

beats.head: Maestro renders the refill webform inline.
beats.lede: Pre-filled with prescriber, pharmacy, last fill date.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for early-refill or interaction flags.
Submit fires — bound to the F1 biometric proof.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK VISUALIZES MAESTRO'S BINDING
scenes.head: Riverside's clinical Agreement Desk shows the auth event and the refill request as one DEA-defensible record.
scenes.lede: The refill request queues for the prescriber's e-signature. Maestro has been binding the events the whole time — Riverside's clinical Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that surfaces the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at session entry, and the refill submission — linked as a continuous identity thread. One audit trail meets DEA's identity-proofing requirement.

beats.head: Clinical Agreement Desk opens to Jordan's session activity.
beats.lede: Auth event + refill submission in a single view.

beats.head: Explicit DEA-grade identity-proofing record.
beats.lede: "Verified by CLEAR · Jordan Kim · State ID ***6118 · 09:03 EST" → "Schedule II refill request · 09:07 EST".

beats.head: Prescriber receives the request with the linked proof.
Pharmacy receives the executed Rx.
beats.lede: Audit trail complete; refill ready for pickup.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE PATIENT PORTAL
scenes.head: Jordan completes new-patient pre-reg in Riverside's portal the night before the visit.
scenes.lede: Jordan Kim opens Riverside Health's patient portal on a phone the night before a first appointment. Demographics, insurance card photo, reason for visit, allergies, current medications.

beats.head: Jordan logs into Riverside's patient portal.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro packages the patient packet from Jordan's selections + insurance verification.
scenes.lede: Submit fires. Maestro pulls the right consent forms based on the visit type, runs an insurance verification (real-time eligibility), packages the patient packet, and routes it back to Jordan for signature.

beats.head: Maestro selects consent set per visit type.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Jordan signs HIPAA + financial responsibility + new-patient consent in one ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packet arrives in seconds — Riverside-branded, three forms in order. Jordan signs in the same portal session.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · CARE RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Riverside's care team picks up Jordan's record in Workspaces.
scenes.lede: Daniela Okafor, the patient coordinator, sees Jordan's pre-reg packet in the care Workspace — already verified, already routed to the EHR. Day-of arrival becomes a 60-second check-in.

beats.head: Care Workspace opens with Jordan's pre-reg.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Diane Park — Mid-Year Change of Circumstance

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Diane logs into Cascade's member portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Diane Park, mid-year on Medicaid, opens Cascade's member portal. Because eligibility data is sensitive and recipient impersonation is a known fraud surface, Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login — biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any change-of-circumstance action. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Diane hits Cascade's member portal login.
Maestro invokes CLEAR for member-portal step-up.
CLEAR session opens on Diane's phone.
Authenticated session opens.
beats.lede: Biometric proof bound to the session. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Diane reports a mid-year address change; Maestro serves the change-of-circumstance webform — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside the authenticated session, Diane opens "Update My Information." Maestro renders the change-of-circumstance webform — new address, effective date, reason — pre-filled with her existing record and the F1 IDV identity binding. No second IDV. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Diane enters new address + effective date.
Submit fires — bound to the biometric proof.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · CASCADE'S AGREEMENT DESK LINKS THE TWO EVENTS
scenes.head: Cascade's case Workspace + Agreement Desk show the auth and the address change as one continuous record.
scenes.lede: Cascade's eligibility engine re-runs the determination from the new address. Maestro has been binding the events all along — Asha's case Workspace surfaces the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at session entry, and the address-change submission — linked as a continuous identity thread. Audit-grade. Defensible against fraud and against benefit-administration audit alike.

beats.head: Case Workspace opens with Diane's session.
Eligibility engine re-runs from the new address.
Asha sees the change in her queue.

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Asha kicks off an out-of-cycle recert via Cascade's internal exception webform.
scenes.lede: An audit hits Diane Park's record and Asha needs to recertify out of cycle. Asha opens Cascade's internal exception webform — member ID, reason-for-recert, new-circumstance details. Selections drive Maestro to assemble a tailored recert packet.

beats.head: She enters member ID + recert reason.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out a tailored exception recert packet.
scenes.lede: Asha submits. Maestro pulls the right verification questions for the audit reason, pre-fills from Cascade's system of record, and routes back to Diane for signature.

beats.head: Maestro selects the exception verification template.
SOR pre-fill across the recert form.
Delivery channel selected per Diane's preference.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Diane signs the exception recert packet — same flow as the auto-fired path.
scenes.lede: The exception packet arrives at Diane's preferred channel. She signs in a Cascade-branded ceremony.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · CASE HISTORY
scenes.head: The exception recert lands in Diane's case Workspace alongside her annual recert record.
scenes.lede: Asha's case Workspace shows both the auto-fired annual recert (from the canonical demo) and this out-of-cycle exception recert in the same activity log. The audit reviewer can trace the decision.

beats.head: Case Workspace opens with Diane's full history.
Activity log shows both Maestro fan-outs.
